guidelines.meta_title: How to Write Great Feedback | Uni Feedback
guidelines.meta_desc: Learn how to write helpful, honest course reviews that actually help other students make better decisions.
guidelines.title: How to Write Great Feedback
guidelines.subtitle: Learn how to write helpful, honest course reviews that actually help other students make better decisions.
guidelines.intro1: Uni Feedback exists so students can help each other make better course choices. Every review you write might be the thing that helps other students decide to take or avoid a course. That's why the quality of what you write matters.
guidelines.intro2: All reviews are anonymous. Nobody will know who wrote what, so feel free to share your real experience without worrying about how it comes across.
guidelines.value_honest_desc: Your review has to reflect your actual experience. Share what you genuinely went through, not what you think sounds good, and not what you think others want to hear.
guidelines.value_helpful_desc: Write as if you're advising a friend who is about to decide whether to take this course. What do they actually need to know?
guidelines.writing_relevant_title: Talk about whatever felt relevant to you.
guidelines.writing_relevant_desc: If something stood out during your time in the course (good or bad), it's probably relevant to other students too. Based on the feedback we've collected, students tend to care about teaching, assessment, materials, and tips. But don't feel limited to these. Cover what actually mattered to you.
guidelines.writing_specific_desc: Vague opinions don't help anyone decide anything. "The midterm covered 3 weeks of content with no practice problems beforehand" is ten times more useful than "midterms were unfair." That includes naming professors when relevant, especially when you're praising someone.
guidelines.writing_personal_desc: Once feedback shifts to impressions of someone's character, it starts losing value. Talk about what happened and how it affected you. The more grounded in real experience your feedback is, the more weight it carries.
guidelines.writing_friend_title: Write like you're talking to a friend.
guidelines.writing_friend_desc: A review that sounds like a student talking to another student is much more useful than a polished, formal one. Remember you're talking to someone who wants to know what this course was like, not for a project report.
guidelines.writing_angry_desc: A review written in frustration rarely gives other students the information they actually need. Step away, breathe in, then write.
guidelines.writing_critical_note: It's fine to be critical. It's fine to give a 1-star rating. We will never remove a review just because it's negative.
guidelines.ai_title: Using AI to write your review
guidelines.ai_desc1: Using AI to help write your review is fine. I often use it myself to organize my thoughts or find the right words for something I want to say. That's totally valid, but keep in mind:
guidelines.ai_point1: Your review has to reflect your experience. If an AI invented details, exaggerated things, or filled in gaps you don't actually know about, that defeats the purpose. Other students are counting on real experiences.
guidelines.ai_point2: Don't over-polish it. This is a platform for students talking to other students. There's no one to impress and no one who'll know it's you. A review that sounds overly formal or corporate loses the warmth that makes it actually useful. Use your own voice, just make sure your thoughts come through clearly.
guidelines.not_allowed_intro: Most of this is common sense, and most people never need to read this list. But just so we're all on the same page:
not_allowed_items: Insults or personal attacks (anything targeting appearance, age, gender, race, religion, or disability)
Profanity or vulgarity (swearing, crude language, sexually explicit content. You know.)
Personal contact information (a professor's home address, personal email, phone number, etc.)
Copying another review (we check for near-exact matches)
Fabricated or AI-generated content (that doesn't reflect a real personal experience)
guidelines.not_allowed_report: If you see a review that violates these guidelines, use the flag button. Don't post a counter-review.
quick_rules: You can only submit one feedback per course
You should have taken or be currently taking the course
We never edit your submission to make it comply. Either it stays as written, or it's removed
If we do remove a review, we'll reach out to explain why and help you update it so it can go up again
guidelines.questions: Got questions or spotted something that shouldn't be here? Reach out at
guidelines.full_guidelines_prefix: For the complete moderation policy, see the

points.meta_desc: Learn how to earn Uni Feedback Points by sharing course reviews and inviting friends.
points.intro_highlight: Points reflect how much you're helping other students.
points.intro_rest: By sharing your feedback and letting other people know about Uni Feedback, you help build a better academic environment for everyone!
points.give_feedback_desc: If your feedback follows our <0>guidelines</0>, you'll earn points based on how helpful it is:
give_feedback_points: +4 points for each feedback category you mention (see below);
+3 bonus points if you cover all 4 categories.
points.give_feedback_cta: Ready to share your experience? <0>Give feedback</0>
points.invite_desc: Bring your classmates to Uni Feedback! When they post their first feedback, you both earn 15 points.
points.invite_from_profile: You can invite friends from your <0>profile page</0>
points.check_points: You can check your current points on your <0>profile page</0>
points.giveaway_bonus_desc: Just for the current <0>giveaway</0>, you can earn extra points two more ways. These add to your entries in the draw, on top of the points above.
points.giveaway_bonus_instagram_desc: Follow <0>@unifeedback</0> on Instagram and link your Instagram on your <1>profile page</1> so we can confirm it.
points.giveaway_bonus_instagram_privacy: Your Instagram is never shown to anyone. It's only used to verify that you follow Uni Feedback. We cross-check follows at the time of the draws.
points.giveaway_bonus_quality_desc: Submit at least 5 feedbacks worth 20 points.
points.categories_desc: We look for these four things because they're what students actually care about. Mention them naturally in your feedback to unlock bonus points. Cover all four to get an extra 3-point bonus!
points.questions: Got questions or something not clear? Email us at <0>afonso@uni-feedback.com</0>, and we'll get back to you!

giveaway.meta_title: Uni Feedback Giveaway: Win €50 at FNAC
giveaway.meta_desc: Share feedback on your courses for a chance to win one of 3 × €50 FNAC gift cards. Ends July 31, 2026.

giveaway_page.hero_subtitle: Tell us what you thought of the courses you took this year. We've got 3 × €50 cards to give away!
giveaway_page.prizes_subtitle: We've got 3 × €50 FNAC cards to give away:
giveaway_page.prizes_random_desc: 2 of the cards go to a random draw. Every point you earn is one entry. The more points you collect, the better your chances.
giveaway_page.prizes_ambassador_desc: 1 card goes to whoever brings the most people to Uni Feedback. Share the invite link on your <profileLink>profile page</profileLink> with your friends and the prize could be yours.
giveaway_page.howto_step1_desc: Use your university email. We'll send a code to that email to confirm it's you.
giveaway_page.howto_step2_desc: Share your thoughts on the courses you took. Good, bad, or "meh". The more detail, the better.
giveaway_page.howto_step3_desc: Every feedback earns you points. The more points you collect, the better your chances of winning.
giveaway_page.points_subtitle: Every point you collect is an entry in the draw: the more you have, the better your chances of winning.
giveaway_page.points_normal_feedback_desc: For each feedback you give. The more complete it is, the more points you earn.
giveaway_page.points_normal_referral_desc: Share the invite link from your <0>profile page</0> with friends. When a friend gives their first feedback, you both earn 15 points.
giveaway_page.points_bonus_instagram_desc: Follow <0>@unifeedback</0> on Instagram and link your Instagram on your <1>profile page</1> so we can confirm it.
giveaway_page.points_bonus_instagram_privacy: Your Instagram is never shown to anyone. It's only used to verify that you follow Uni Feedback. We cross-check follows at the time of the draw.
giveaway_page.points_bonus_quality_desc: Submit 5 feedbacks worth 20 points and you earn 100 extra points.
giveaway_page.faq_intro_prefix: Here are the most common questions about the giveaway. For all the details, check the

faq_items.question: Until when can I enter?
faq_items.answer: You can earn points until **July 31**. After that, the giveaway closes and we pick the winners.

faq_items.question: How are the winners chosen?
faq_items.answer: There are 3 × €50 FNAC prizes. **2 are drawn at random** (each point gives you one entry in the draw) and **1 is guaranteed** for whoever brings the most friends (the "Top Ambassador").

faq_items.question: How do I enter and earn more points?
faq_items.answer: Each feedback is worth up to 20 points (more detail = more points) and every friend you invite earns +15 points for each of you — you and them — when they post their first feedback. There are also two bonuses: +20 for linking your Instagram and +100 if you submit at least 5 feedbacks worth 20 points. See it all on the [points page](/en/points).

faq_items.question: Where can I see my points?
faq_items.answer: On your [profile](/en/profile) you can see your total points and every feedback you've submitted.

faq_items.question: Which points count for the draw?
faq_items.answer: Feedback points for **2025/2026** courses and referral points earned between **June 29 and July 31** count, plus the Instagram and quality bonuses. Points you earned in the February giveaway also count. Feedback for older courses still earns permanent points on your profile, but those don't count toward this draw.

faq_items.question: I submitted feedback before. Does it count?
faq_items.answer: It does! As soon as you log in, every feedback you've already submitted with that email is credited automatically. You don't need to do anything else.

faq_items.question: Can Erasmus or international students participate?
faq_items.answer: Yes! If you have a valid email and you're currently enrolled, you're in!

faq_items.question: Why do I need to use my university email?
faq_items.answer: To ensure all feedback is legitimate and comes from real students. This protects the quality of the platform for everyone.

faq_items.question: Is my feedback really anonymous?
faq_items.answer: 100% yes. Professors and other students will never see your name or email attached to your reviews. We only use your email to verify you're a real student and to contact the winners.

faq_items.question: When will the winners be announced?
faq_items.answer: The giveaway ends on **July 31 at 23:59**. We'll contact the winners via university email and announce them on our [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/unifeedback/) between **August 1 and 2**. Check your spam folder!

giveaway_page.cta_subtitle: Submit your first feedback and start earning points!

giveaway_recap.hero_subtitle: The giveaway ended on February 27th. Here's what we accomplished
giveaway_recap.thanks_p1: Words can't express how happy it makes me to see so many students joining this initiative and helping each other.
giveaway_recap.thanks_p2: Together, we're building a platform that will help <0>thousands of students</0> know how courses really are, make better choices, and prepare better for their semester.
giveaway_recap.thanks_p3: The feedback you shared is helping students make better decisions today, and will continue guiding them in the years to come.
giveaway_recap.thanks_signoff: A huge thanks to everyone who contributed!!
giveaway_recap.winner_message: Congratulations, I hope you enjoy the concert 🎸🔥
giveaway_recap.top_contributor_suffix: high-quality feedbacks for all the courses they took since their first year at Uni! What a legend!!
giveaway_recap.peak_activity_prefix: Our busiest day was February 23rd, when we collected
giveaway_recap.cta_subtitle: Even without prizes, your feedback still matters.

giveaway_rules.meta_desc: Official rules for the Uni Feedback Summer 2026 Giveaway: eligibility, how to enter, points and prizes (3 × €50 FNAC).
giveaway_rules.intro: This page sets out the official rules for the Uni Feedback Summer 2026 Giveaway. By taking part, you agree to the rules below.
giveaway_rules.eligibility_status_desc: You must be a student in a degree available on Uni Feedback and have a valid institutional email address.
giveaway_rules.eligibility_account_desc: You must log in to Uni Feedback using your institutional email address.
giveaway_rules.eligibility_feedback_desc: To be eligible to win, you must have submitted at least one approved feedback during the giveaway period. Participants who have not submitted any feedback are not eligible for the draw, regardless of points earned through other means.
giveaway_rules.eligibility_one_desc: Only one account per student is allowed. Multiple accounts, shared accounts, or variations of the same email address will result in disqualification.
giveaway_rules.period_desc1: The giveaway runs from June 29 to July 31, 2026, 23:59:59 (Lisbon time).
giveaway_rules.period_desc2: Feedback submitted after the deadline is welcome, but does not count toward the giveaway.
giveaway_rules.period_winner: Winners are selected and contacted between August 1 and 2, 2026.
giveaway_rules.enter_feedback_desc: To enter, you must submit at least one feedback for a course you took during the 2025/2026 academic year.
giveaway_rules.enter_guidelines_prefix: Your feedback just needs to follow our
giveaway_rules.enter_existing_desc: You don't need to do anything special: as soon as you log in, all the feedback you've submitted with that email is credited to your profile.
giveaway_rules.points_intro: Your points are your chances in the draw: the more points you collect, the more chances you have to win.
points_feedback_items: +4 points for each category covered: Teaching, Assessment, Materials and Tips
+3 bonus points if you cover all 4 categories
giveaway_rules.points_instagram_desc: Follow us on Instagram (<0>@unifeedback</0>) and link your Instagram on your profile to earn +20 points. Your profile is private: your Instagram is never shown publicly. We verify follows before the draw.
giveaway_rules.points_quality_desc: Submit at least 5 feedbacks worth 20 points each for 2025/2026 courses to earn +100 extra points.
giveaway_rules.points_referral_desc: When a friend you invited submits their first approved feedback, you both earn +15 points — you and them. No cap. You can invite friends from your profile page.
giveaway_rules.points_which_count: Which points count: feedback for 2025/2026 courses and referrals made between June 29 and July 31, plus the Instagram and quality bonuses. The points you earned in the February giveaway are also valid in this giveaway.
giveaway_rules.points_more_prefix: Learn more about the points system
giveaway_rules.prizes_split_intro: The 3 prizes are awarded in two formats:
giveaway_rules.prizes_draw_desc: A random draw where each point is one chance. More points = more chances.
giveaway_rules.prizes_recruiter_desc: Awarded deterministically to the student with the most successful referrals during the giveaway period (Jun 29–Jul 31). A referral is successful when the friend signs up and gets their first feedback approved. In case of a tie, the winner is whoever completed their last referral earliest.
prizes_rules_items: No one wins more than one prize. We award the Best Ambassador first, then draw the 2 remaining prizes among the other participants (without replacement).
Before the draw, the participants with the most points are audited for fraud or AI-generated content.
giveaway_rules.winner_notification_desc: Winners are notified via their university email address.
giveaway_rules.winner_announcement_desc: For transparency, and with the winner's consent, we announce them as [First Name] [Last Initial] from [Faculty] (e.g. João M. from IST). All course feedback remains 100% anonymous, whether you win or not.
giveaway_rules.winner_claim_desc: The winner must reply to the notification email within 7 days. If the prize is not claimed, a backup winner is selected. The prize is awarded as is and has no cash alternative.
giveaway_rules.fraud_content_desc: Plagiarized, AI-generated, low-effort, or spam feedback is not allowed and receives 0 points.
giveaway_rules.fraud_referral_desc: Self-referrals or any attempt to manipulate the referral system will result in immediate disqualification.
giveaway_rules.fraud_review_desc: Uni Feedback reserves the right to review, audit, and disqualify accounts at any point, before or after the draw, if abuse or rule violations are detected.
giveaway_rules.final_privacy_prefix: For information about how your data is handled, please see our
giveaway_rules.final_decisions: All decisions related to feedback approval, point allocation, disqualification, and winner selection are final.
giveaway_rules.final_questions: Got any other questions about the giveaway? Email us at <0>afonso@uni-feedback.com</0>.

unsubscribe.invalid_desc: This unsubscribe link is invalid. Please use the link from your email.
